Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: 3-D printed ultrastrong heat-resistant aluminum alloy achieved by bioinspired nanoscale amorphous confinement

Measured_Composition.Measurement_Method: gas atomization powder composition reported in Methods

Composition_Info.Note: TiB2 nanoparticles (2 wt%) mixed as nucleating agents; balance Al calculated from nominal formula Al-8.5Fe-1.3V-1.7Si. Minor impurities C, S, O, N reported for powder feedstock.

Process_Text.original: Gas atomized FVS0812 powder (15-53 um, D50=34.3 um) mixed with 2 wt% TiB2 nanoparticles (50-100 nm) by mechanical stirring in anhydrous ethanol at 1000 rpm for 3 h, dried at 60C for 48 h. LPBF printed using EP-M150 Metal 3D Printer under argon atmosphere. Laser power 150 W, scan speed 1000 mm/s, scan spacing 0.08 mm, layer thickness 0.02 mm. Build platform preheated to 200C.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF of FVS0812 powder with 2 wt% TiB2 nucleating agents; 150 W, 1000 mm/s, 0.08 mm hatch, 0.02 mm layer, Ar atmosphere, 200C build platform

Process_Info.Equipment: EP-M150 Metal 3D Printer (Hangzhou Eplus 3d Tech, China)

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed grains (~1.6 um) with cellular dendrite structure. alpha-Al cells (~67 nm diameter) interwoven with continuous amorphous cell boundaries (~14 nm wide, 38% fraction). Grain boundaries and cell boundaries form continuous network enriched in Fe and Si. TiV nucleation cores (~50 nm) enriched with Ti and V embedded in grain centers. Al8Fe2Si precipitates (~1-2 um, ~1%) at grain boundaries. 3D crystalline-amorphous interpenetrating network structure.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed fine grains (1.6 um) with alpha-Al cellular structure and continuous amorphous Fe/Si-enriched cell boundaries (14 nm wide, 38 vol%). TiV nanoparticles (12 nm) within cells. Al8Fe2Si precipitates at grain boundaries.

Properties_Info.Note: Room temperature tensile test; highest UTS among 3D-printed Al alloys by ~100 MPa

Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: cylindrical, gauge dia 3 mm, gauge length 40 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Creep rate 1-3 orders of magnitude lower than other Al alloys

Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: cylindrical, gauge dia 3 mm, gauge length 40 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Creep rate at least 50% lower than reference materials at 300C

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; 0.1 um indentation depth; 0.05 um/s loading rate; 1 um spacing
at 298.15 K; 0.1 um indentation depth; 0.05 um/s loading rate; 1 um spacing
Properties_Info.Note: Heat-affected zone hardness; lower than fusion zone due to coarser microstructure

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: After 100 h thermal exposure at 573.15 K (300C)
Properties_Info.Note: Retains >80% of original strength after prolonged thermal exposure

Composition_Info.Note: Same alloy as as-printed after 100 h thermal exposure at 300C. Minor differences due to partial crystallization of amorphous boundaries.

Process_Text.original: Same LPBF-printed FVS0812+TiB2 alloy after thermal exposure at 300C for 100 h in air. Minor microstructural evolution including partial crystallization of amorphous boundaries and formation of Al12(Fe,V)3Si nanocrystals in heat-affected zones.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF FVS0812+TiB2 after 100 h thermal exposure at 573.15 K (300C); partial crystallization of amorphous phase

Microstructure_Text.original: After 100 h at 300C: grain size increased marginally to ~1.95 um. Fusion zone fraction decreased to ~63%. alpha-Al cells retained similar size (~72 nm). Continuous amorphous network partially preserved in fusion zone. Heat-affected zones show network-like interwoven structure with Al12(Fe,V)3Si nanocrystals at cell boundaries; nanoscale gaps compromise network integrity. Al8Fe2Si precipitates retained.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: After thermal exposure: slightly coarser grains (1.95 um), partial amorphous phase crystallization, network structure partially preserved with Al12(Fe,V)3Si nanocrystals in HAZ

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; after 100 h at 573.15 K thermal exposure
Properties_Info.Note: Retains >85% of as-printed average hardness after thermal exposure

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: After 100 h at 573.15 K thermal exposure
Properties_Info.Note: Yield strength and UTS remain above 80% of original values after prolonged exposure
